title: Chicken tagine with preserved lemon
subtitle: Moroccan braised chicken with olives, saffron and preserved lemon.
excerpt: Chicken thighs braised in a saffron and ginger broth with green olives and preserved lemon, the sauce reducing to a fragrant, complex glaze. A Moroccan classic made in a tagine or a casserole. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Moroccan chicken tagine with preserved lemon and olives. The dish is a central recipe of Moroccan cooking, documented by Paula Wolfert in Couscous and Other Good Food from Morocco (1973) and Claudia Roden in The Food of the Eastern Mediterranean. Preserved lemons are a staple of Moroccan and North African cooking.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze for up to two months. The preserved lemon flavour mellows further on reheating.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Excellent made the day before. Reheat gently on the hob.

recipeTools.notes: Use a casserole if no tagine is available.

content.text: Preserved lemon is the ingredient that cannot be properly substituted in this dish. Its texture is soft and silky, its flavour simultaneously salty, sour and intensely lemony in a way fresh lemon is not. It takes one month to make and can be bought in most supermarkets. Use only the rind; rinse it briefly to reduce the saltiness and discard the pulp.

items.prepNote: preserved; rinse, discard pulp, slice the rind
taste first; preserved lemon adds salt

content.text: Brown the chicken and build the base

Heat {{olive-oil}} of olive oil in a tagine or casserole over medium-high heat. Season {{chicken-thigh}} chicken thighs lightly with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Brown skin-side down for 4 minutes; remove. In the same pan, cook {{onion}} grated onion over medium heat for 8 minutes. Add {{garlic}} of grated garlic, {{ginger-root}} of grated ginger, {{ras-el-hanout}} of ras el hanout and {{turmeric}} of turmeric; stir for 2 minutes.

Return the chicken to the pan. Pour in {{stock-chicken}} of stock and the saffron with its soaking water. Bring to a simmer, cover and cook over low heat for 35 minutes. Add {{olives-green}} of green olives and the rinsed preserved lemon rind. Cook uncovered for 15 minutes until the sauce has reduced and the chicken is very tender. Scatter {{coriander}} of coriander to serve.

items.cause: Preserved lemon not rinsed, or seasoned before tasting.
items.fix: Always rinse the preserved lemon rind and taste before adding any additional salt. Preserved lemons are heavily salted in the curing process.

items.cause: Lid kept on throughout, preventing reduction.
items.fix: Remove the lid for the last 15 minutes. The sauce should reduce to a concentrated, glossy consistency that coats the chicken.

items.symptom: Missing the distinctive preserved lemon flavour
items.cause: Pulp included rather than just the rind.
items.fix: Use only the rind; scrape out and discard the pulp. The rind carries the flavour; the pulp adds bitterness.

content.text: The tagine is the earthenware cooking vessel of Morocco and North Africa, with a conical lid that returns steam as condensation to the base, keeping the contents moist throughout a long cook. The dish named after the pot is a whole category of slow braises: chicken, lamb and vegetables with preserved lemons, olives and an assortment of warm spices. This chicken version is the most accessible version for the uninitiated.
